data.title: HydrogenPro ASA - Invitation to Q4 2024 webcast
data.description:
HydrogenPro ASA - Invitation to Q4 2024 webcast

HydrogenPro ASA - Invitation to Q4 2024 webcast

PR Newswire

OSLO, Norway, Feb. 19, 2025

OSLO, Norway, Feb. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- HydrogenPro ASA (OSE: HYPRO) will publish the financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024 on Tuesday 25 February 2025 at 07:00 CET. The company will host a webcast presentation of the results the same day at 10:00 CET, followed by a Q&A session. The presentation will be held by Jarle Dragvik (CEO) and Martin Thanem Holtet (CFO). Questions can be posted online during the presentation. Link to webcast: https://channel.royalcast.com/landingpage/hegnarmedia/20250225_5/. The presentation will be held in English. The report and presentation will be made available at www.hydrogenpro.com and www.newsweb.no. For further information, please contact:

Martin Thanem Holtet (CFO)

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Wednesday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Occidental Stock Drops After Mixed Results. What's Dragging on Buffett's Favorite.

OXY stock has lost 19% over the past year. Weak crude prices are to blame. 

 
Oil prices end higher after drone attack on Russian pipeline

Oil futures were lifted Tuesday, ending with gains after a drone attack by Ukraine on a pipeline carrying crude from Kazakhstan across southern Russia curtailed flows. 

 
Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash. 

 
Galp Misses Expectations; Launches Buyback

The oil-and-gas producer launched a $262 million share buyback and said its results reflected a strong performance despite a volatile economic environment. 

 
Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Gain insight on Repsol, SK Innovation, Seatrium, and more in the latest Market Talks covering the Energy and Utilities sectors. 

 
New Sanctions for Putin's 'Shadow Fleet' Are on the Horizon

The EU is ready to roll out new sanctions on vessels transporting illicit oil, but the Trump administration has yet to say if it will participate. 

 
Clean-Energy Program Fueled by Biden Takes Deep Staff Cuts Under Trump

The number of employees in the Energy Department's loan office has been sliced by roughly 25%. 

 
Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary

The energy company plans to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise as much as $3 billion through a listing that would be one of Japan's biggest such offerings in recent years. 

 
Trump Anxiety Could Boost Sales at These Renewables Companies

The rush to purchase renewable energy equipment before clean energy laws get changed could juice the results of companies that make that equipment. 

 
IEA Slightly Lifts Oil Demand Outlook, Says Supply Surplus Narrowing

The International Energy Agency modestly raised its forecast for global oil-demand growth and said improved compliance with output quotas among members of the OPEC+ alliance is reducing a projected supply surplus in the market.

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

February 19, 2025 04:15 ET (09:15 GMT)

data.title: Treasury yields nudge higher after hotter U.K. inflation
data.description:
By Jamie Chisholm

Bond yields held near their highs for the week after U.S. President Trump pledged a raft of import tariffs and inflation in Britain jumped to a 10-month high.

What's happening

-- The yield on the 2-year Treasury BX:TMUBMUSD02Y dipped 0.2 basis points to 4.312%. Yields move in the opposite direction to prices.

-- The yield on the 10-year Treasury BX:TMUBMUSD10Y rose 0.9 basis points to 4.565%.

-- The yield on the 30-year Treasury BX:TMUBMUSD30Y added 0.9 basis points to 4.786%.

What's driving markets

Government bond yields in Europe rose, helping to underpin their U.S. peers, after a report showed U.K. annual headline inflation jumped to 3% in January from 2.5% in December.

Economists had expected a 2.8% reading and the hotter figure dovetails with recent evidence from the U.S. that inflationary pressures appear to be building again in many developed economies.

"Inflation is back to behaving like a caffeinated flea, bouncing higher in January, after December's bigger-than-expected fall," said Sarah Coles, head of personal finance at Hargreaves Lansdown.

"The bounce was even bigger than the market had expected, so while it's not going to set off a cacophony of alarm bells at the Bank of England, it's not going to make them any more enthusiastic about rate cuts in the immediate future either," Coles added.

The U.K. 10-year gilt yield BX:TMBMKGB-10Y rose 4 basis points to 4.599% and the equivalent maturity German bund yield BX:TMBMKDE-10Y added 2 basis points to 2.520%.

Concerns about stubborn price pressures in the U.S. were expressed by San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly on Tuesday, when she said monetary policy needs to remain restrictive until more progress is made on inflation.

Comments from U.S. President Donald Trump late Tuesday that he may soon impose 25% tariffs on auto, semiconductor, and pharmaceutical imports - which could increase inflation - appeared to have been shrugged off by investors.

Potential market catalysts due Wednesday for traders to consider include:

-- 8:30 a.m. Eastern. U.S. housing starts for January.

-- 8:30 a.m. U.S. building permits for February.

-- 10:30 a.m. Atlanta Fed GDPNow first quarter update.

-- 1:00 p.m. Treasury announces result of $16 billion auction of 20-year bonds.

-- 2:00 p.m. Minutes of the Fed's January Federal Open Committee Meeting.

-- 5:00 p.m. Fed Vice Chair Philip Jefferson speaks at Vassar College.

-Jamie Chisholm

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-19-25 0401ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: Elon Musk's X reportedly in talks for funding at same valuation as original Twitter deal
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

Elon Musk is reportedly looking to raise money for the social media service X that he owns at the same price he paid, reluctantly, for Twitter in 2022.

Bloomberg News reported the deal talks for X though it said key details could still change and the financing round could even end.

If successful, it would be a remarkable change in fortune. Fidelity has been marking down its valuation of the shares it holds in X at a 70% discount to the $44 billion that Musk paid for the service after a Delaware judge forced him to make good on his offer.

However, big banks earlier this month wrapped up the sale of $5.5 billion of debt backed by X at 97 cents on the dollar, a lower discount, and bigger size, than they originally planned, according to the Wall Street Journal.

The WSJ reported that X's financials have changed. While its revenue dropped sharply, to $2.7 billion last year from $5 billion in 2021, it's making nearly twice as much money, using a measure of profitability called adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

X also holds a 10% stake in xAI, Musk's artificial-intelligence venture that just rolled out the Grok 3 large language model.

X's larger rival, Facebook and Instagram owner Meta Platforms (META), has rallied this year, its shares surging 22%.

Trump Media & Technology (DJT), the operator of the Truth Social platform where President Donald Trump posts, by contrast has seen its stock fall 14% this year.

-Steve Goldstein

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  02-19-25 0406ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: U.S. Futures Up, European Stocks Flat as Trump Floats Tariffs on Autos, Chips, Pharma
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were up 0.1% and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbed 0.1%. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 was flat in morning trading. STMicroelectronics added 5.9% and Antofagasta rose 4%. On the other hand, Koninklijke Philips slipped 7.5%, and Recordati dropped 6.4%. The FTSE 100 lost 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were mixed as France's CAC 40 decreased 0.1% and Germany's DAX rose 0.3%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index fell 0.1% to 101.2.

In commodities, Brent crude rose 0.6% to $76.29 a barrel, and WTI crude gained 0.6% to $72.27 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 1.3% to 49.86 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield rose by 3 basis points to 2.525% from 2.5%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury climbed by 1 basis point to 4.567% from 4.556%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index declined 0.3%, and Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 0.1%, whereas China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.8%. 




  -This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

data.title: Sibanye-Stillwater Enhances Marikana Contract, Signs Chrome Agreements
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon 




  Sibanye-Stillwater  said it made enhancements to its Marikana Contract and signed a new Chrome Management Agreement with the Glencore-Merafe Venture. 



  The South African mining company said Wednesday that the pact is expected to optimize value from the production of chrome. 



  The company said it partnered with various third parties, including the Glencore-Merafe Venture, to recover and market chrome produced by its South African operations for platinum group metals. Chrome is an important byproduct of the production of such metals, and its South African operations are a significant producer of global chrome ore. 



  The enhancements to the Marikana Contract are expected to accelerate the delivery of contracted chrome volumes by approximately 20 years, the company said. This will result in an increase in future cash flow, it said. 





  Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 19, 2025 03:50 ET (08:50 GMT)

data.title: Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market to Reach USD 5070.9 Million by 2030 Amid Rising Sustainability Efforts | Future Market Insights, Inc.
data.description:
The Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market is set to grow at a 7.6% CAGR from 2025 to 2035, reaching USD 5070.9 million by 2030, driven by strict environmental regulations and rising demand for sustainable materials across industries.

NEWARK, DE / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / The Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market is expected to experience robust expansion over the next decade, fueled by increasing awareness regarding the environmental hazards posed by conventional plasticizers. Bio-plasticizers, derived from renewable sources such as vegetable oils, starch, and other bio-based feedstocks, are gaining traction as industries transition toward sustainable alternatives.

Market projections estimate that the Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market will reach USD 5070.9 million by 2030, growing at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 7.6% from 2025 to 2035. Various end-use industries, including construction, automotive, and packaging, are incorporating bio-plasticizers to comply with stringent regulations and meet sustainability goals.

Discover key market opportunities - Request your sample report now! https://www.futuremarketinsights.com/report-sample#5245502d47422d2e3230303931

Emerging Market Trends Driving Growth in the Germany Bio-Plasticizers Industry

  • How Strict Environmental Regulations Are Shaping the Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market: Germany has established a stringent regulatory framework to curb the environmental impact of conventional plasticizers. The European Union's REACH (Registration, Evaluation, Authorization, and Restriction of Chemicals) regulation has played a crucial role in limiting the use of harmful phthalate-based plasticizers, creating a demand for bio-based alternatives. Furthermore, the German government continues to promote eco-friendly materials through incentives and tax benefits, propelling the bio-plasticizers market forward.

  • Growing Demand for Eco-Friendly Building Materials Drives Market Expansion: The construction industry is witnessing a paradigm shift toward sustainable building materials, driving the demand for bio-plasticizers. Flooring, wall coverings, and sealants now increasingly incorporate bio-based plasticizers due to their reduced toxicity and minimal carbon footprint. Germany's ambitious green building standards and certifications, such as the DGNB (German Sustainable Building Council), have further accelerated the adoption of bio-plasticizers in the construction sector.

  • Sustainable Vehicle Manufacturing: A Key Driver for Bio-Plasticizer Adoption: Germany, a global leader in automotive manufacturing, is embracing bio-plasticizers to enhance sustainability in vehicle production. Leading automotive brands are integrating bio-plasticizers in interior applications, such as dashboards, upholstery, and wiring, to reduce dependency on fossil fuel-derived materials. The push for electric vehicles (EVs) and circular automotive supply chains is further strengthening the bio-plasticizers market within the sector.

  • Breakthroughs in Bioplastics: Advancing the Future of Sustainable Materials: The bioplastics industry is experiencing significant advancements in terms of material performance and scalability. Companies are developing innovative bio-plasticizer formulations with enhanced durability, flexibility, and thermal stability, making them viable alternatives to traditional plasticizers. The rise in R&D investments and collaborations between bio-based product manufacturers and industrial end-users is further fueling market growth.

  • Circular Economy Initiatives and Waste Reduction Strategies in Germany: Germany's commitment to a circular economy is encouraging industries to adopt sustainable raw materials and waste-reducing solutions. Bio-plasticizers contribute to this vision by offering biodegradable and recyclable properties, aligning with the country's goals of minimizing plastic waste and reducing carbon emissions. Companies are actively exploring closed-loop recycling systems to enhance the sustainability profile of bio-plasticized products.

  • Industry-Wise Growth and Emerging Trends in the Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market: The bio-plasticizers market in Germany is expanding across multiple industries, including packaging, construction, healthcare, and textiles. Packaging manufacturers are integrating bio-based plasticizers to create flexible, biodegradable packaging solutions. The healthcare industry is also adopting non-toxic bio-plasticizers for medical devices and pharmaceutical packaging. Additionally, the textile sector is utilizing bio-plasticizers in synthetic fibers and coatings, reducing its environmental impact.

"The Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market is poised for significant growth, driven by stringent environmental regulations and the rising demand for eco-friendly materials. Industry players are heavily investing in sustainable solutions to cater to evolving consumer preferences and regulatory standards," says Nikhil Kaitwade, Associate Vice President at Future Market Insights (FMI)

Growth Prospects and Market Innovations in Germany's Bio-Plasticizers Sector

  • Navigating Germany's Evolving Bio-Plasticizer Regulations: Government regulations will continue to play a vital role in shaping the Germany Bio-Plasticizers Market. Stricter norms on plastic waste management and eco-labeling requirements will drive innovation and adoption of bio-based alternatives.

  • Driving Sustainability with Bio-Based Plasticizers: With Germany emphasizing sustainable urban development, the demand for eco-friendly building materials will surge. Bio-plasticizers will be a key component in achieving energy-efficient and environmentally responsible construction practices.

  • Innovations Shaping the Future of Bio-Plasticizers: Innovations in bio-based polymer science will enhance the performance and cost-effectiveness of bio-plasticizers. New processing techniques and material engineering breakthroughs will improve their functionality in diverse applications.

  • Accelerating Sustainability in Germany's Auto Industry: The shift toward electric and hybrid vehicles will encourage automakers to integrate bio-plasticizers in lightweight and durable vehicle components. This trend will reinforce Germany's leadership in sustainable automotive solutions.

data.title: HUTCHMED to Announce 2024 Final Results
data.description:
HONG KONG and SHANGHAI and FLORHAM PARK, N.J., Feb. 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- HUTCHMED (China) Limited (“HUTCHMED”) (Nasdaq/AIM: HCM; SEHK:13) will be announcing its final results for the year ended December 31, 2024 on Wednesday, March 19, 2025 at 7:00 am Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) / 11:00 am Greenwich Mean Time (GMT) / 7:00 pm Hong Kong Time (HKT).

Analysts and investors are invited to join a conference call and audio webcast presentation with Q&A, conducted by HUTCHMED management.

The English conference call and audio webcast will take place at 8:00 am EDT / 12:00 pm GMT / 8:00 pm HKT on Wednesday, March 19, 2025. In addition to the usual English webcast, there will also be a Chinese (Putonghua) webcast at 12:30 am GMT / 8:30 am HKT on Thursday, March 20, 2025 (8:30 pm EDT on Wednesday, March 19, 2025). Both webcasts will be available live via the company website at www.hutch-med.com/event/. The presentation will be available for downloading before the conference call begins. Details of the conference call dial-in will be provided in the financial results announcement and on the company website. A replay will also be available on the website shortly after the event.

About HUTCHMED

HUTCHMED (Nasdaq/AIM:HCM; HKEX:13) is an innovative, commercial-stage, biopharmaceutical company. It is committed to the discovery and global development and commercialization of targeted therapies and immunotherapies for the treatment of cancer and immunological diseases. Since inception, HUTCHMED has focused on bringing drug candidates from in-house discovery to patients around the world, with its first three medicines marketed in China, the first of which is also approved in the US, Europe and Japan. For more information, please visit: www.hutch-med.com or follow us on LinkedIn.

data.title: Sinch Delivers Charging and Policy Control for Huge NXTGN Digital MVNE Stack in South African Market
data.description:
Sinch Delivers Charging and Policy Control for Huge NXTGN Digital MVNE Stack in South African Market

Sinch Delivers Charging and Policy Control for Huge NXTGN Digital MVNE Stack in South African Market

PR Newswire

STOCKHOLM, Feb. 19, 2025

STOCKHOLM, Feb. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Sinch (Sinch AB (publ)) – (XSTO: SINCH), which is pioneering the way the world communicates through its digital Customer Communications Cloud, is pleased to provide critical real-time charging, policy control, and messaging solutions which has enabled Huge NXTGN to launch its advanced digital VNE platform.

Huge NXTGN's digital VNE platform empowers businesses in South Africa to deliver value-added digital connectivity services, enabling them to create distinctive customer experiences. The platform significantly reduces go-live time to just a few weeks, compared to the months traditionally required.

Huge NXTGN, a Johannesburg-based leader in digital and embedded communications services and part of the publicly listed Huge Group Limited's investment portfolio, is revolutionizing the Virtual Network Enabler (VNE) landscape in South Africa. By enabling businesses of all sizes—micro, mini, and large—to seamlessly launch VNO services on-demand, Huge NXTGN empowers brands to enhance their strategies while delivering greater value and a differentiated customer experience compared to existing mobile offerings.

Sinch's carrier grade cloud platform delivers scalable, fast and flexible online policy and charging for voice, data, messaging, fraud and security, and digital services solutions to meet MVNOs' requirements. Sinch also provides a range of services to help operators drive customer A2P Monetization, firewalls, analytics and reporting solutions for telecom operators with full suite of professional and managed services.

Nicklas Molin, EVP, Market Unit International at Sinch stated, "We are excited to partner with Huge NXTGN to deliver the value of communications and digital services to businesses, while protecting operators' networks & their subscribers. This collaboration delivers innovation and improved services to businesses via MVNO offering and we are dedicated to support our clients in delivering such advanced solutions and better experiences to consumers."

Jason Harmsen Chief Executive Officer, Huge NXTGN said: "Our growth strategy leverages an innovative digital enablement ecosystem that empowers brands of all sizes to seamlessly integrate and launch connectivity services aligned with their unique strategies. By lowering barriers to entry, we redefine accessibility, helping businesses transform their propositions and deliver unmatched value to customers. This ecosystem represents a game-changing approach in the South African market, making advanced digital connectivity solutions available to brands faster and more efficiently. Our partnership with Sinch underscores our commitment to delivering advanced, best-of-breed solutions that support our partners and ensure their success through innovation and exceptional customer experiences."

For more information about Sinch and its comprehensive suite of communication solutions, please visit Sinch.com

For more information, please contact:
Janet Lennon, Director of Global PR & Communications
janet.lennon@sinch.com

Craig Atherfold, Public Relations and Communications for Huge NXTGN
Craig.A@corporateimage.co.za

This information was brought to you by Cision http://news.cision.com

data.title: U.K. Inflation Reaches 10-Month High, Complicating Bank of England's Rate Path — 2nd Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl 




  U.K. annual inflation in January reached its highest level since March last year, complicating the picture for the Bank of England after it said it would cut interest rates gradually amid a weak outlook for economic growth. 



  Consumer prices were 3.0% higher in January than a year earlier, up from 2.5% in December, the Office for National Statistics said Wednesday. Economists had expected annual inflation to rise to 2.8% according to a poll compiled by The Wall Street Journal. 



  That meant inflation rose further above the bank's 2% target, suggesting the BOE will find it more difficult to bring interest rates down in the hopes of boosting an economy that grew 0.1% in the final quarter of 2024. 



  The bank cut its key rate to 4.5% at its latest meeting earlier in February, with Governor Andrew Bailey saying it would take a "gradual and careful" approach to reducing rates ahead. The BOE has cut rates at a slower pace than both the Federal Reserve and the European Central Bank since the summer. 



  However, the uptick in inflation was expected by BOE policymakers. January's increase in inflation was a result in a jump in air fares, as well as private-school fees, which became subject to value-added tax at the start of the year. 



  Wage data published Tuesday also showed accelerating pay growth, which will likely knock on to inflation, particularly in the labor-intensive services sector. 



  Services inflation surged to 5.0% in January, up from 4.4% in December, while core prices that exclude more volatile food and energy were up 3.7%, from 3.2% in the last month of 2024. 



  The climb in inflation will be uncomfortable for the BOE, though not enough to prevent it from cutting interest rates further, Capital Economics deputy chief U.K. economist Ruth Gregory said in a note to clients. 



  "But it will mean it continues to cut rates only slowly," she added. 



  Inflation is expected to rise even further, peaking at 3.7% by the end of summer on the back of higher energy costs, the bank said in its latest macroeconomic forecasts. 



  They were the same forecasts that slashed economic-growth expectations for 2025--to 0.75% from 1.5% previously--symbolizing the contrasting concerns of stubborn inflation and anemic growth for the bank. 



  Furthermore, the uncertainty of trade tariffs from the Trump administration threatens to derail attempts to put the economy on a firmer footing. BOE's Bailey said Tuesday that he would prefer less volatility from the U.S., and that fragmentation of trade was bad for global growth. 



  Trade concerns could also further weaken the pound against the U.S. dollar, which would push prices of energy and other imports higher. The Federal Reserve has also given no suggestion it is in a rush to lower borrowing costs, suggesting investors will stick with the currency offering higher yield returns. 





  Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  February 19, 2025 03:22 ET (08:22 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
